# Blueprint v0.2.1 — first real packaged release

v0.2.0 attempted the same package set but the build pipeline failed
on a missing NSIS toolchain on the Windows runner. v0.2.1 ships the
fix and is the first release with installable artifacts.

Everything noted in the v0.2.0 release notes still applies — see
[`0.2.0.md`](./0.2.0.md) for the full feature summary. v0.2.1 is the
same code with a working release pipeline:

- `windows-latest` runner now installs NSIS via Chocolatey before
  `wails build -nsis` runs.
- NSIS path added to `$GITHUB_PATH` so subsequent steps find
  `makensis.exe`.

## Install

**Windows.** Download `blueprint-windows-amd64-installer.exe`, run
it. Admin elevation required to register the Windows Service.

**Linux.** Download `blueprint-svc-linux` + `install-linux.sh`.
`sudo bash install-linux.sh` registers the systemd unit. Desktop UI:
download `blueprint-linux-amd64`, run.

**macOS.** Download `blueprint-macos-universal.app.zip`, extract,
open.

## Known limitations carried over from v0.2.0

- Windows installer is unsigned in this release; SmartScreen will
  show a warning. Signing certificate landing in v0.2.2.
- The license-verification public key in `internal/license/license.go`
  is still the dev placeholder. Replace before going commercially live.
- Engine-plan compilation for TensorRT-LLM is not wrapped; Blueprint
  loads pre-built `.engine` plans produced externally with
  `trtllm-build`.
